original_image_tags: Black-and-white photograph of the front of a dark-colored envelope. The envelope has handwritten and stamped text in white ink. Postage stamps are affixed to the top right corner. The address is in formal cursive script.

elements.description: A photograph of a dark-colored envelope. The envelope has handwritten and typed text in white ink. In the top left corner, the return address reads: 'Miguel Angel Garcia Macias. Descubridor e Inventor Ideografo. Av. Pino Suarez #324. Veracruz. Ver.' The main address is written in a mix of cursive and block letters: 'Al C: Presidente de la Comision de Investigacion Cientifica de Estados Unidos de Norte America. U.S. Court House Foley Sq. Nueva York.' In the top right corner, there are four postage stamps from Mexico. Two are rectangular with the text 'SERVICIO AEREO' and circular postmarks dated '20 ABR 50-19'. The other two are smaller, featuring an eagle illustration, with the text 'CORREO AEREO' and 'MEXICO 25 centavos', and postmarks dated '20 MAR 50-19'. The envelope appears aged and slightly worn.

judge.reason: Description B quotes specific verbatim text for stamps and addresses that Description A does not see at all, and they disagree on whether text is 'handwritten and stamped' (A) versus 'handwritten and typed' (B).
